en.extract:
The ornate fruit dove (Ptilinopus ornatus) is a species of bird in the family Columbidae. It is found in New Guinea. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest and subtropical or tropical moist montane forest.


Taxonomy and systematics
The ornate fruit dove was originally described as Ptilopus ornatus (an incorrect spelling of Ptilinopus) by Hermann Schlegel in 1871 on the basis of specimens from New Guinea. The species' generic name comes from the Ancient Greek ptilon (feather) and pous (foot), while the specific name ornatus is from the Latin word ornatus, meaning ornate or decorated. Ornate fruit dove is the official common name designated by the International Ornithologists' Union (IOU).
The ornate fruit dove is one of over 50 species of pigeon in the fruit dove genus Ptilinopus, which is found throughout Southeast Asia and Oceania. A 2014 study of mitochondrial and nuclear DNA by Alice Cibois and colleagues found that the ornate fruit dove was most closely related to the orange-fronted fruit dove. The same study also found that these two species were further sister (the closest relative of) to Wallace's fruit dove. These three species are sister to the pink-spotted fruit dove and these four species are sister to the grey-headed fruit dove. This group of five species is sister to a clade formed by the orange-bellied, white-headed, and claret-breasted fruit doves. The Tanna fruit dove is sister to all other species in the group. The following cladogram shows the relationships of the ornate fruit dove with other species in its group based on the 2014 study:


Subspecies
The IOU currently recognises two subspecies of the ornate fruit dove. These two species are sometimes recognised as separate species by other authorities.

P. o. ornatus Schlegel, 1871: The nominate subspecies, it is found in the Bird's Head Peninsula in northwestern New Guinea.
P. o. gestroi D'Albertis & Salvadori, 1875: Also known as the Eastern ornate fruit dove or Gestroi's fruit dove, it was originally described as a separate species by Luigi D'Albertis and Tommaso Salvadori. It is found from the Fakfak and Kumawa Mountains on the Onin Peninsula east to Milne Bay, including the Cyclops Mountains. It has a mustard-yellow crown, compared to the nominate subspecies's wine-red crown. Populations from the Onin Peninsula to the Chimbu Province are sometimes treated at a distinct subspecies kaporensis, but differences between P. o. gestroi and P. o. kaporensis are mostly clinal and do not warrant different subspecies.


References

The '''ornate fruit dove''' ('''''Ptilinopus ornatus''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Columbidae]]. It is found in [[New Guinea]]. Its natural [[habitat]]s are [[subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest]] and subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]].

== Taxonomy and systematics ==
The ornate fruit dove was originally [[Species description|described]] as ''Ptilopus ornatus'' (an incorrect spelling of ''[[Ptilinopus]]'') by [[Hermann Schlegel]] in 1871 on the basis of specimens from [[New Guinea]].<ref>{{Cite journal|last1=Schlegel|first1=Hermann|last2=Koninklijk Zoologisch Genootschap Natura Artis Magistra te Amsterdam|year=1873|title=Observation Zoologiques. V|url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/49085561#page/64/mode/1up|journal=Nederlandsch Tijdschrift voor de Dierkunde|language=nl|location=Amsterdam|volume=4|page=52}}</ref> The species' [[Generic name (biology)|generic name]] comes from the [[Ancient Greek]] ''ptilon'' (feather) and ''pous'' (foot), while the [[Specific name (zoology)|specific name]] ''ornatus'' is from the [[Latin]] word ''ornatus'', meaning ornate or decorated.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Jobling|first=James A.|title=Helm Dictionary of Scientific Bird Names|publisher=[[Christopher Helm]]|year=2010|isbn=978-1-4081-3326-2|location=London|pages=284, 322|language=en}}</ref> Ornate fruit dove is the official [[common name]] designated by the [[International Ornithologists' Union]] (IOU).<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|editor-last=Gill|editor-first=Frank|editor2-last=Donsker|editor2-first=David|editor3-last=Rasmussen|editor3-first=Pamela|title=Pigeons|url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/new/bow/pigeons/|access-date=2022-01-21|website=IOC World Bird List}}</ref>

The ornate fruit dove is one of over 50 species of pigeon in the fruit dove genus ''Ptilinopus'', which is found throughout [[Southeast Asia]] and [[Oceania]].<ref name=":0" /> A 2014 study of [[Mitochondrial DNA|mitochondrial]] and [[nuclear DNA]] by Alice Cibois and colleagues found that the ornate fruit dove was most closely related to the [[orange-fronted fruit dove]]. The same study also found that these two species were further [[Sister group|sister]] (the closest relative of) to [[Wallace's fruit dove]]. These three species are sister to the [[pink-spotted fruit dove]] and these four species are sister to the [[grey-headed fruit dove]]. This group of five species is sister to a [[clade]] formed by the [[Orange-bellied fruit dove|orange-bellied]], [[White-headed fruit dove|white-headed]], and [[claret-breasted fruit dove]]s. The [[Tanna fruit dove]] is [[Sister group|sister]] to all other species in the group. The following [[cladogram]] shows the relationships of the ornate fruit dove with other species in its group based on the 2014 study:<ref>{{Cite journal|last1=Cibois|first1=Alice|last2=Thibault|first2=Jean-Claude|last3=Bonillo|first3=Céline|last4=Filardi|first4=Christopher E.|last5=Watling|first5=Dick|last6=Pasquet|first6=Eric|date=2014|title=Phylogeny and biogeography of the fruit doves (Aves: Columbidae)|url=https://linkinghub.elsevier.com/retrieve/pii/S1055790313003412|journal=[[Molecular Phylogenetics and Evolution]]|language=en|volume=70|pages=442–453|doi=10.1016/j.ympev.2013.08.019|pmid=24012584 |bibcode=2014MolPE..70..442C |url-access=subscription}}</ref>

   |2={{clade
    |1=[[White-headed fruit dove]] (''P. eugeniae'')
    |2=[[Claret-breasted fruit dove]] (''P. viridis'') }} }}
  |2={{clade
   |1=[[Grey-headed fruit dove]] (''P. hyogastrus'')

    |2={{clade
     |1=[[Wallace's fruit dove]] (''P. wallacii'')

      |1=[[Orange-fronted fruit dove]] (''P. aurantiifrons'')
      |2='''Ornate fruit dove''' (''P. ornatus'') }} }} }} }} }} }}
|style=font-size:95%; line-height:95%;|label1=''tannensis'' group}}

=== Subspecies ===
The IOU currently recognises two subspecies of the ornate fruit dove.<ref name=":0" /> These two species are sometimes recognised as separate species by other authorities.<ref name=":2">{{Cite book|last1=Beehler|first1=Bruce|title=Birds of New Guinea: Distribution, Taxonomy, and Systematics|last2=Pratt|first2=Thane|publisher=[[Princeton University Press]]|year=2016|isbn=978-1-4008-8071-3|location=[[Princeton, New Jersey]]|pages=81–82|language=en|oclc=936447561}}</ref>

* ''P. o. ornatus'' <small>[[Hermann Schlegel|Schlegel]], 1871</small>: The [[nominate subspecies]], it is found in the [[Bird's Head Peninsula]] in northwestern New Guinea.<ref name=":0" />
* ''P. o. gestroi'' <small>[[Luigi D'Albertis|D'Albertis]] & [[Tommaso Salvadori|Salvadori]], 1875</small>: Also known as the Eastern ornate fruit dove<ref>{{Cite web|title=''Ptilinopus ornatus gestroi'' (Ornate Fruit-Dove (gestroi))|url=https://avibase.bsc-eoc.org/species.jsp?lang=EN&avibaseid=E0D24D45&sec=summary&ssver=1|access-date=2022-02-04|website=[[Avibase]]}}</ref> or Gestroi's fruit dove,<ref name=":2" /> it was originally described as a separate species by [[Luigi D'Albertis]] and [[Tommaso Salvadori]].<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Museo civico di storia naturale di Genova|year=1875|title=Catalogo di una collezione di uccelli dell' Isola Yule e della vicina costa meridionale della penisola orientale ella Nuova Guinea, raccolti da L. M. D'Albertis|trans-title=Catalog of a collection of birds from Yule Island and the nearby southern coast of the eastern peninsula of New Guinea, collected by L. M. D'Albertis|url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/item/107646#page/840/mode/1up|journal=Annali del Museo Civico di Storia Naturale di Genova|language=it|location=[[Genova]]|publisher=Tip. del R. Istituto Sordo-Muti|volume=7|pages=834–835}}</ref> It is found from the [[Fakfak Mountains|Fakfak]] and [[Kumawa Mountains]] on the Onin Peninsula east to [[Milne Bay]], including the [[Cyclops Mountains]]. It has a mustard-yellow {{Birdgloss|crown}}, compared to the nominate subspecies's wine-red crown. Populations from the Onin Peninsula to the [[Chimbu Province]] are sometimes treated at a distinct subspecies ''kaporensis'', but differences between ''P. o. gestroi'' and ''P. o. kaporensis'' are mostly [[Cline (biology)|clinal]] and do not warrant different subspecies.<ref name=":2" />

zh.extract:
丽色果鸠（学名：Ptilinopus ornatus），是鸽形目鸠鸽科的一种鸟类。


特征
丽色果鸠体重约163克，翼长约150.7毫米，嘴峰长约18.6毫米，喙宽度约4毫米，喙厚度约4.5毫米，跗蹠长约23.6毫米，尾长约71.7毫米。丽色果鸠在其分布区内为留鸟，其栖息在半开放的高大的树木组成的森林中，食性为草食性，主要食物来源是水果。


亚种
P. o. ornatus：分布于新几内亚西北部（阿尔法克山和沿海的福格科普地区）。
P. o. gestroi：分布于新几内亚（西至独眼巨人山和奥宁半岛）。
